title: OPERATION — PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS

figures_detected.description: Two graphs showing CVT speed change characteristics. Left graph: Engine revolution (rpm) vs Vehicle speed (km/h) comparing N.CVT (solid line, MAX and MIN bands) with Manual transmission gear speeds (1st through 5th, overdrive, dashed lines). Right graph: Engine revolution (rpm) vs Vehicle speed showing MAX line, Ds range region, Ds (MIN) and D (MIN) lines.

content_blocks.text:
The speed change characteristic of the is not linear (like that of a manual transmission vehicle, but can be expressed as the region bounded by the maximum and minimum pulley ratios.
In the graph, the MAX line shows the relationship between engine speed and vehicle speed at full acceleration: the minimum line shows the same relationship when accelerating slowly.

steps.text:
"D" Position.
Normal driving mode, from starting off to maximum speed.
Speed change occurs between 1600 and 4000rev/min depending upon gradual or fast acceleration.
When cruising speed is reached and pressure on the accelerator pedal is reduced (transmission enters overdrive), the pulley ratio is at maximum.
If the pedal is pressed while cruising – ie to accelerate – the pulley ratio shifts to minimum and vehicle speed rises quickly.

"Ds" Position.
In the "Ds" mode, the speed (ratio) range is limited to the higher zone of the speed change region. "Ds" also provides an engine braking effect.

"R" Position.
In the reverse mode, the pulleys are locked in "low", and the speed (ratio) change does not occur.

"P" Position.
In the park position, the output (secondary) pulley is locked mechanically.

"N" Position.
In this position, the forward and reverse changeover is locked in a neutral state.

items.label: Speed change range (D position)
items.condition: depending upon gradual or fast acceleration

model_notes: Applies to N.CVT (Nissan CVT) equipped K11 Micra. CVT speed change characteristic compared with manual transmission gear speeds (1st–5th speed, overdrive). Ds range provides limited higher-zone speed change region with engine braking.
